message:
fix(focus): purge excluded ids from FocusManager.history so focus_back can't resurrect closed modals

When a modal stack popped a modal and asked the focus manager to
repair itself via `repair_focus_after_excluding_ids`, we rewrote
`current` if it pointed into the excluded set and we popped the
focus trap, but we never cleaned up `history`. That meant the
back-stack still held focus ids that no longer existed, and a
subsequent `focus_back()` could quietly hand focus to a widget
belonging to a popped modal — either a dangling reference (if the
id had been reused by a new widget) or, more commonly, focus
"snapping" to something that was visibly gone from the screen.

Fix:

- crates/ftui-widgets/src/focus/manager.rs: in
  `repair_focus_after_excluding_ids`, start by
  `self.history.retain(|id| !excluded.contains(id))`. This runs
  before the existing current/blur repair logic, so by the time we
  check whether to pop a trap we also know the history is clean.
  The rest of the function is unchanged.
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/modal/focus_integration.rs: regression
  test `pop_discards_closed_modal_focus_history`. Setup: three
  focus nodes (ids 1, 2, 3); focus 1, push a modal that traps
  focus to {2, 3} and focus 3 (which triggers history entries
  for 1 and 3 under the trap), then pop the modal. Assertions:
  after pop, `current()` is 1 (correct — we return to the
  pre-trap focus), and `focus_back()` must return false (no prior
  entry in history), and `current()` must stay at 1. The pre-fix
  code left id 3 in history so `focus_back()` would return true
  and `current()` would become 3 — pointing at a node that the
  popped modal had been trapping.

Focus-adjacent behavior (trap push/pop, excluded-id clearing of
`current`, pending-blur propagation) is untouched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
